page.title: Marbella Sunset Tapas Cruise: A Romantic Evening on the Sea

data.meta_description: Experience a romantic Marbella sunset tapas cruise from Puerto Banús. Enjoy 2 hours on a luxury yacht with drinks, snacks & sea views. Book your evening now.
data.summary: A romantic Marbella sunset tapas cruise offers couples an intimate evening on the sea with drinks, light bites, and stunning coastal views from Puerto Banús. Perfect for anniversaries, proposals, or a special date night.
key_facts: 2-hour sunset cruise on a luxury yacht
Includes drinks (cava, beer, wine, soft drinks) and light tapas-style snacks
Prices from €749 for up to 9 guests (Astondoa 40) or 11 guests (Azimut 39)
Available year-round, best in late spring to early autumn

faq.q: What is included in a Marbella sunset tapas cruise?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light tapas-style snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% Spanish IVA. You just bring yourselves and your camera.

faq.q: How long does the sunset tapas cruise last?
faq.a: Our standard sunset cruise is 2 hours, typically departing 1.5 hours before sunset. This gives you plenty of time to enjoy the golden hour, toast with cava, and watch the sun dip behind La Concha mountain.

faq.q: Which boats are available for a romantic sunset cruise?
faq.a: For a romantic evening, we recommend the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, up to 9 guests) or the Azimut 39 (12.5m, up to 11 guests). Both depart from Puerto Banús and offer comfortable deck space and a cabin for privacy.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food or drinks?
faq.a: We already include drinks and light snacks. If you have specific dietary requirements or want to bring a special bottle of champagne, you're welcome to do so – just let us know in advance.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Your safety is our priority. If the weather forecast shows strong winds or rough seas, we'll contact you to reschedule or offer a full refund. We monitor conditions closely and rarely cancel.

faq.q: Is the sunset cruise suitable for a proposal?
faq.a: Absolutely. Many guests choose our sunset cruise for proposals. We can arrange a special bottle of cava, decorate the boat, or coordinate with a photographer on the dock. Just tell us your plans.

We cruise along the Marbella coastline, passing the Golden Mile, Cabopino, and often heading towards Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. The route is flexible – your skipper will choose the best spot for sunset views.

faq.q: How do I book a Marbella sunset tapas cruise?
faq.a: Book directly via our website or WhatsApp. We require a 50% deposit to secure your date, with the balance due 48 hours before departure. For last-minute bookings, full payment is needed.

data.body_html:
Imagine gliding across the Mediterranean as the sun sinks behind the Sierra Blanca, a glass of chilled cava in hand, the sky turning shades of amber and rose. A Marbella sunset tapas cruise is the quintessential romantic evening – intimate, effortless, and unforgettable. Whether you're celebrating an anniversary, planning a proposal, or simply craving a date night with a difference, our 2-hour sunset charter from Puerto Banús delivers exactly that.

Why a Sunset Tapas Cruise is the Ultimate Romantic Evening

There's something magical about watching the day fade from the water. The crowds thin, the heat softens, and the coastline of Marbella – from the Golden Mile to Cabopino – takes on a golden glow. On a private yacht, you skip the restaurant queues and the noise. Instead, you get uninterrupted views, gentle sea breezes, and the company of your favourite person. It's intimate, it's personal, and it's yours alone.

What's Included in Your Sunset Cruise

Every charter comes with a licensed skipper, fuel, and a selection of drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. We also provide light tapas-style snacks – think olives, nuts, cured meats, and cheeses – perfect for nibbling as you cruise. Insurance and 21% Spanish IVA are all included. You don't need to worry about a thing.

Choose Your Yacht for the Evening

For a romantic sunset cruise, we recommend our Tier A boats – the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, up to 9 guests) or the Azimut 39 (12.5m, up to 11 guests). Both depart from Puerto Banús and offer comfortable deck space, a cabin for privacy, and plenty of room to lounge. Pricing is straightforward: €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours if you want to extend into a full evening. All prices include skipper, drinks, snacks, and VAT.

For larger groups or a more luxurious experience, browse our full fleet of yachts in Marbella.

The Route: Where You'll Cruise

Your skipper will tailor the route to the evening's conditions, but typical sunset cruises head west from Puerto Banús, passing the iconic Marbella Marina, the Golden Mile, and the beaches of Cabopino. You might stop for a swim at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés if the light allows. The goal is always to find the best vantage point for the sunset – often near the cliffs of Cabopino or just off the coast of Estepona.

When to Book Your Sunset Cruise

The best months for a sunset cruise are May through October, when temperatures are warm and the sun sets late (around 21:00 to 21:30). Spring and autumn offer quieter seas and equally stunning skies. We recommend booking at least a week in advance, especially for weekends and peak season. Charter times are set to depart 1.5 hours before sunset, so you get the full golden hour experience.

Make It Extra Special: Proposals and Celebrations

Many couples choose our sunset cruise for proposals. We can arrange a bottle of premium cava, decorate the boat with petals, or coordinate with a photographer to capture the moment on the dock. Just let us know your plans when you book. For anniversaries or birthdays, we can also arrange a small cake or a bouquet of flowers. It's the little touches that make the evening memorable.

What to Bring and What to Wear

Dress for the evening: a light jacket or wrap is useful as the breeze picks up after sunset. Comfortable shoes with non-slip soles are ideal. Don't forget your camera or phone for photos – the sunset views are spectacular. Sunglasses and sunscreen are still a good idea even in the late afternoon. And if you have a favourite playlist, ask your skipper if you can connect via Bluetooth – most boats have sound systems.

How to Book Your Marbella Sunset Tapas Cruise

Booking is simple. Visit our yacht charter page to check availability, or message us on WhatsApp for a quick response. We require a 50% deposit to secure your date, with the balance due 48 hours before departure. For last-minute bookings, full payment is needed. We accept credit cards, bank transfers, and PayPal. Once booked, you'll receive a confirmation with meeting instructions at Puerto Banús.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is included in a Marbella sunset tapas cruise?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light tapas-style snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% Spanish IVA. You just bring yourselves and your camera.

How long does the sunset tapas cruise last?

Our standard sunset cruise is 2 hours, typically departing 1.5 hours before sunset. This gives you plenty of time to enjoy the golden hour, toast with cava, and watch the sun dip behind La Concha mountain.

Which boats are available for a romantic sunset cruise?

For a romantic evening, we recommend the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, up to 9 guests) or the Azimut 39 (12.5m, up to 11 guests). Both depart from Puerto Banús and offer comfortable deck space and a cabin for privacy.

Can we bring our own food or drinks?

We already include drinks and light snacks. If you have specific dietary requirements or want to bring a special bottle of champagne, you're welcome to do so – just let us know in advance.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Your safety is our priority. If the weather forecast shows strong winds or rough seas, we'll contact you to reschedule or offer a full refund. We monitor conditions closely and rarely cancel.

Is the sunset cruise suitable for a proposal?

Absolutely. Many guests choose our sunset cruise for proposals. We can arrange a special bottle of cava, decorate the boat, or coordinate with a photographer on the dock. Just tell us your plans.

Where does the cruise go?

We cruise along the Marbella coastline, passing the Golden Mile, Cabopino, and often heading towards Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. The route is flexible – your skipper will choose the best spot for sunset views.

How do I book a Marbella sunset tapas cruise?

Book directly via our website or WhatsApp. We require a 50% deposit to secure your date, with the balance due 48 hours before departure. For last-minute bookings, full payment is needed.
